events.title: Heavy rainfall causes flash flooding in the U.S. Atlantic Southeast (June 1–3, 1997)
events.description: Heavy rainfall from June 1–3, 1997 affected parts of Florida, Georgia, Virginia, and West Virginia and produced flash flooding. A USGS National Water Conditions summary for June 1997 describes very high rainfall rates and notes flood-related deaths in West Virginia.

location.name: United States (Florida, Georgia, Virginia, West Virginia)

events.title: Charleston, South Carolina flash flood from extreme rainfall (June 6, 1997)
events.description: On June 6, 1997, extreme rainfall in the Charleston area caused a major flash-flood event. A USGS National Water Conditions summary for June 1997 reports 7.37 inches of rain in downtown Charleston and notes many road closures.

key_quotes.quote: ...7.37 inches of rain in downtown Charleston...

events.title: Flooding across Indiana and Ohio from heavy thunderstorms (June 1–3, 1997)
events.description: Heavy thunderstorms during June 1–3, 1997 caused flooding across parts of Indiana and Ohio. A USGS National Water Conditions summary for June 1997 describes impacts on multiple rivers and continued flooding effects in Indiana later in the month.

key_quotes.quote: Heavy thunderstorms... caused flooding in Indiana and Ohio...

events.title: South-central Texas floods from heavy rainfall (June 21–22, 1997)
events.description: Heavy rainfall on June 21–22, 1997 caused severe flooding across parts of south-central Texas. A USGS fact sheet describes damaging flooding on rivers and creeks and notes federal disaster declarations for multiple counties.

sources.headline: Floods in South-Central Texas, June 1997 (Fact Sheet 053-98)

events.title: Tropical Storm Andres (Eastern Pacific) and rainfall impacts near Central America (June 1–7, 1997)
events.description: Tropical Storm Andres occurred in the Eastern Pacific during June 1–7, 1997. NOAA rainfall documentation and tropical cyclone reporting pages track the storm and related rainfall near its path toward Central America.

location.name: Eastern Pacific / Central America (near Mexico to El Salvador)

events.title: Soufrière Hills dome collapse triggers major pyroclastic flows (Montserrat)
events.description: A lava-dome collapse at Soufrière Hills Volcano generated large pyroclastic flows that traveled down multiple valleys on Montserrat, causing fatalities and widespread damage. The reports describe significant inundation and destructive impacts associated with the collapse and flows.

key_quotes.quote: major lava-dome collapse generated... pyroclastic flows

dedupe.cluster_hint: Soufriere Hills 1997-06-25 dome collapse pyroclastic flows

events.title: Pilot reports steam at Wrangell Volcano summit (Alaska)
events.description: A pilot reported steam rising from the summit area of Wrangell Volcano in Alaska. The Alaska Volcano Observatory assessed the observation as fumarolic steam and noted it can be mistaken for eruptive activity.

key_quotes.quote: pilot report described steam rising from the summit

events.title: Steaming and greenish snow discoloration reported near Kukak/Snowy Mountain (Katmai region, Alaska)
events.description: A U.S. Coast Guard helicopter crew reported steaming from vents and greenish discoloration of snow on a peak in the Katmai region of Alaska. The Alaska Volcano Observatory characterized the observation as hydrothermal or fumarolic activity rather than an eruption.

location.name: Kukak Volcano / Snowy Mountain area (Katmai region), Alaska, USA

key_quotes.quote: reported greenish snow discoloration and steaming from vents

events.title: Kīlauea east rift zone eruption continues (USGS update, June 13, 1997)
events.description: USGS reported continued activity in Kīlauea's long-running east rift zone eruption (Episode 55), including vent and lava-flow activity around the Puʻu ʻŌʻō area. The update describes ongoing lava emplacement affecting areas near the eruptive vents.

sources.headline: Volcano Watch — Kilauea Eruption Update June 13, 1997

key_quotes.quote: continuing activity in... east rift zone eruption (Episode 55)

events.title: Earthquake strikes western Japan with reported disruptions (Yamaguchi/Shimane area)
events.description: A news report described a strong earthquake striking parts of western Japan, with strong shaking felt in areas including Shimane and nearby prefectures. The report referenced disruptions including a train incident after striking a rock and mentions consistent with rockfall or slope impacts.

location.name: Northern Yamaguchi Prefecture / Shimane area (Masuda), Japan

sources.headline: Strong earthquake strikes areas in western Japan

key_quotes.quote: Strong earthquake strikes areas in western Japan

events.title: Around 50 forest fires reported in Northern Ontario; reinforcements and water bombers deployed
events.description: A news report described roughly 50 active forest fires in Northern Ontario, with reinforcements and water bombers deployed as hot, dry winds contributed to fire activity.

sources.headline: UPI Focus: Some 50 forest fires ablaze in Ontario

key_quotes.quote: Some 50 forest fires ablaze in Ontario

events.title: GOES satellite analysis documented smoke plumes from lightning-initiated fires in Ontario and Quebec
events.description: A satellite analysis page documented multiple smoke plumes and fire hot spots linked to lightning-induced wildfires in Ontario and Quebec, with transported smoke visible over parts of the Great Lakes and New England.

sources.headline: GOES satellite imagery (1997-06-10) showing smoke plumes/fire hot spots

key_quotes.quote: smoke plumes and fire hot spots

events.title: USFWS final rule lists guajón frog (Eleutherodactylus cooki) as threatened
events.description: A U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service final rule listed the guajón frog (Eleutherodactylus cooki) as a threatened species under the Endangered Species Act. The rule cites threats including development and associated infrastructure impacts within the species’ restricted range in Puerto Rico.

sources.headline: Endangered and Threatened Wildlife and Plants; Determination of Threatened Status for the Guajon (Eleutherodactylus cooki)

key_quotes.quote: determination of threatened status for the guajon (Eleutherodactylus cooki)

dedupe.cluster_hint: USFWS ESA listing final rule (Federal Register)

events.title: USFWS final rule lists Lessingia germanorum (San Francisco lessingia) as endangered
events.description: A U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service final rule listed Lessingia germanorum (San Francisco lessingia) as an endangered species under the Endangered Species Act. The rule notes threats including habitat loss and fragmentation and other disturbances affecting its limited habitat on the San Francisco Peninsula in California.

sources.headline: Endangered and Threatened Wildlife and Plants; Determination of Endangered Status for Lessingia germanorum (San Francisco Lessingia)

key_quotes.quote: determination of endangered status for Lessingia germanorum (San Francisco lessingia)

dedupe.cluster_hint: USFWS ESA listing final rule (Federal Register)

events.title: USFWS final rule lists four northern California vernal pool plants as endangered
events.description: A U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service final rule listed four vernal pool-associated plant taxa as endangered under the Endangered Species Act. The rule cites habitat loss and degradation affecting vernal pools and seasonally wet areas in northern California as key threats.

sources.headline: Endangered and Threatened Wildlife and Plants; Determination of Endangered Status for Four Plants From Northern California Vernal Pools and Mesic Areas

key_quotes.quote: determination of endangered status for four plants from northern California vernal pools

dedupe.cluster_hint: USFWS ESA listing final rule (Federal Register)

events.title: EPA final rule establishes framework for National Low Emission Vehicle (NLEV) Program
events.description: A GAO product summarizes that EPA published a final rule establishing the main regulatory framework for the National Low Emission Vehicle (NLEV) program, allowing auto manufacturers to voluntarily meet stricter tailpipe emission standards for certain light-duty vehicles and trucks.

key_quotes.quote: final rule providing the main regulatory framework for the National Low Emission Vehicle (NLEV) program

events.title: EPA final rule updates hazardous waste testing/monitoring methods (SW-846 Update III)
events.description: EPA issued a final rule amending hazardous waste regulations for testing and monitoring by adding new and revised analytical methods (Update III to SW-846) and removing obsolete methods referenced in SW-846 and related RCRA regulations.

sources.headline: List of Federal Register notices pertaining to hazardous waste

key_quotes.quote: amending hazardous waste regulations for testing and monitoring activities by adding new and revised analytical methods (Update III to SW-846)

dedupe.cluster_hint: SW-846 Update III final rule June 1997

events.title: DROPPED: EPA final rule vacates certain carbamate hazardous waste listings
events.description: Dropped because the provided sources do not include a citable Federal Register or equivalent primary document confirming the June 17, 1997 final rule as summarized.

events.title: EPA grants immediate final authorization of revisions to Maine’s hazardous waste program under RCRA
events.description: EPA issued an immediate final rule approving revisions to Maine’s authorized hazardous waste management program under RCRA, granting final authorization for specified program revisions, with an effective date contingent on receipt of adverse comments.

key_quotes.quote: immediate final rule approving revisions to Maine’s authorized hazardous waste program under RCRA
